please regard this is my review/opinions only*

I was referred by my sister to the law firm. She talked really highly about it and I thought that maybe reaching out to them, after a traumatic car accident with injuries would be a good idea.

For one, Mr. Okusanya is quick to answer phone calls, patient, and expresses empathy. However, what I've noticed is that there is mal communication between secretary's and attorneys which made my experience more stressful than it should have.

My boyfriend and I were in a car accident and we initially reached out and informed the secretary of our incident and injuries. All of the information will be forwarded to Mr. Okusanya. A week later he followed up and explained how he has "too many cases and to look for another attorney" in the meantime they review my case. A week went by, they followed up, and said "Yes" they will take on my case.

Great! I was finally able to get the justice I deserved.

We filled our consent forms and all, sent it to him, I kept updating their secretary about letters my insurance company were sending me, and following up with Mr. Okusanya. He clearly stated not to communicate with the insurance company and that he will handle the total loss claim.

I admit, when Mr. Okusanya would call. I was usually unable to respond because I am at work Monday through Friday from 8am-4:30pm My phone is on silence. BUT I made sure to follow up and respond in a timely matter.

Now, Mr. Okusanya reviewed my case and said he is forming a team to take it on. My boyfriend called again a week later to the office, and they said they're still reviewing the case and to send my lease agreement form. OK. I sent it. Followed up if they received it.. yes they did. Great. I'm waiting for the next step!

Mr. Okusanya managed to speak to my insurance company and handle a few things, but NEVER followed up with me about the conversation.

They finally assigned someone on the case named Charles James. Asked me to fill out a questionnaire and draw a picture of the incident. I did all that, but now he became aggressive during conversations. No empathy whatsoever and mentioned how I needed to update him every time the insurance reaches out to me and why it takes me so long to respond back. Well, as a client, im not allowed to speak to my insurance, YOU guys represent me. I communicated with their secretary regarding all the letters they sent me and the secretaries always said the information will be forwarded to the attorneys.

I have communicated with them since a day after my accident. I do not understand how he can blame me for something like this when the office is incredibly unorganized and could have updated him.

My boyfriend and I have done all we can to follow up weekly. ONE WEEK (thanksgiving week) I don't respond, Mr. Charles James has the audacity to say I am unresponsive? He did not know anything about my case and asked me if I lived in Orange County!

Sir, I told the office many times I lived in Pomona.

Anyways, the firm is unorganized, attorney James is not empathic to your case, will try to blame you for not knowing things.

One good thing that came out of Mr. James is his advice that the case between two people involved in an accident will be separate. He then said  "best of luck" and professionally said my case is not going to go well and will not continue with my case but is happy to provide advice.

Okay. I understand. Maybe next time, the firm should try being more organized. This would have been avoided.

I would say, don't waste your time here. There are so many good lawyers that are organized, empathetic, and will fight for YOU.
One of the best lawyers in Inland Empire. He knows his work and can be trusted to handle your case with utmost integrity. Well done.
Helped out a lot. Very very smart and gets straight to the point of what is the right thing to do. Work was done well.
OK
Excellent service, very friendly!

What started as a bleak situation turned into something much more hopeful. He was able to gain some ground with the judge, and the outcome was far better than I had expected.

I’m incredibly grateful I made the change. Having Kirk and Valentina on my side made all the difference.

reviews.text:
As a new immigrant navigating the complexities of setting up a trust, I was fortunate to have Ronald Martin III recommended to me by a thoughtful neighbor. After reaching out to 4-5 other attorneys, I found myself overwhelmed with conflicting advice due to my unique circumstances. However, from our very first conversation, Ronald stood out. He took the time to thoroughly explain the pros and cons of each option, ensuring I chose the path that was truly right for me.

What impressed me most was his commitment to clarity and education. Ronald provided me with clear, concise materials that helped demystify the entire trust process, making me feel confident and informed every step of the way. When the trust was finalized, he didn’t just stop there—he guided me through the critical next steps, such as transferring bank accounts into the trust’s name, with patience and precision.

The firm representative that they sent, was no help AT ALL. He claimed that it was out of his hands, and all they could do was wait. Then ghosted my family & left the building.

DO NOT give this firm your money. They are unreliable, unprofessional, & untrustworthy. I understand that because of the recent change in the law that happened on 9/6/25, the process has become a lot more difficult. So I spoke with Cierra’s firm a few days ago. Admittedly, I chose to go with this law firm to support a fellow black woman, I read a bit of her story and it moved me. Now I’m grateful I didn’t chose her…
Red flags:
1. THEY’RE LYING. There’s no free consultation, it’s an intake to gather your info for them to categorize the case in their system and to also find the best point of contact to make sure you send them the retainer. I’ve had previous lawsuits, cases etc and they’re the only law firm I’ve dealt with that didn’t speak with me directly. They didn’t care about my experience.
2. The woman that I spoke with on the phone, very briefly was actually extremely unprofessional. It was as if she was interning. You wouldn’t expect someone, who I assume would have an education to be in her position,  to come off as if they’re not sure what they’re doing and can’t get anything right. It felt like talking to my toddler, trying to get her to understand what I’m saying it was a little frustrating.
3. There’s no empathy or compassion, they just want your money. At least pretend you care lol.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Heaven,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re very sorry to hear that your experience with our team did not meet your expectations.

To clarify, we do not advertise our intake calls as full legal consultations. Our process begins with a brief intake to better understand your legal needs and to determine whether our firm is the right fit before any retainer is requested. This is a common practice in law firms to ensure we’re efficiently allocating resources and serving clients effectively.

We also take professionalism very seriously. If you felt that the person you spoke with was unprepared or dismissive, that’s deeply concerning and not aligned with our standards. We will be reviewing your interaction internally to ensure improvements are made.

Most importantly, we strive to lead with compassion. If we missed the mark in making you feel heard and supported during what we understand is a stressful time, we sincerely apologize.

If you’re open to it, we would appreciate the opportunity to learn more and see how we might make things right. You’re welcome to contact our operations team directly at operations@cierraesqlaw.com.

Wishing you the very best as you move forward.

Warm regards,
Cierra Esq.
Founder & Managing Attorney
Law Office of Cierra Esq.

reviews.text: I’m going to be transparent regarding my experience with Cierra as my attorney. She took in my case two weeks prior to the hearing which was awesome.  She is down to earth and always responsive. She easily grasped and transcribed all court orders given by the judge. I was also blessed that the retainer fee was a fixed amount. There were a few cons hiring her however. She does not read the materials such as any RFOs or declarations filed but a separate person reads this material and prepares Cierra for the court hearings. She also reassured me multiple times that she would be fully prepared for my hearing but when she represented me, she made the assumption that we would be referred to mediation but we were not. That caused so much stress for me since she was not fully prepared. She also asked me questions regarding the case materials when all that information should have been shared with her.  She also notified me last minute that she would be late to the hearing. Her team also filed paperwork last minute but luckily it did get filed in time. The proof of service was not filed however. That also caused so much stress.  I also was not updated on the status of my case throughout the process. I felt more stress hiring Cierra but I can’t say I regret hiring her since she was effective in representing me. I felt better having her by my side than being alone.
Called and made an appointment for a consultation and the Lawyer never called me. Instead, at the end of the day, the appointment was canceled. So disappointing.

reviews.text: Seeing his picture, he looked a little fresh and most likely a new attorney. However, he held his ground and was able to get me a better result than someone I know that was going through similar things but, had an older “seasoned” attorney. I was advised by friends to go for a younger attorney. The difference I find is the younger guys are way more motivated and WILL fight for you. It’s common sense, they are building their reputation and don’t want that jeopardized. The older guys tend to seem too busy, arrogant and just want a check (you know what I mean if you’ve dealt with attorneys). I was dealing with a b/s warrant that he got recalled immediately. He also, gave me the opportunity to not be traditionally silenced in court and thrown in jail for weeks to wait for a hearing based on their “fair system”. He was fairly priced and very thorough. Kept me out of jail. Explained everything in a language I could understand too. I would definitely suggest him (and do*) to family and friends. 5 stars!
reviews.ownerResponse:
It was a pleasure assisting and getting everything back o track. Also, I am ecstatic that we were able to get the results that we were targeting. I had no expectations when I called for a consult in July of this year since lemon law claims are known to be difficult and drawn out processes. However, Lemon Law Experts made it as easy as it could be and I am rid of my lemon and in a new vehicle.

Every step included detailed instructions and consisted simply of collecting/sending documents, signing the agreement from the manufacturer, and surrendering the vehicle. They kept expectations reasonable - stating they would try their hardest but not promising a result. Even if the claim had not been accepted, I would have felt they did everything that could have been done.

Great company, friendly staff, easy processes. If you are looking for someone to help you with your claim, this is who you should use.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Heather! We’re happy to hear the process was straightforward and that our team kept expectations realistic while supporting you every step of the way. Your trust means a lot to us!

reviews.text:
When my brand new Mercedes AMG started having issues and the dealer and manufacturer refused to take responsibility, I knew I was being given the runaround. Frustrated and unsure of what to do, I decided to look for a lemon law firm to help me. My case had tough facts, and after calling several firms who wouldn’t take my case, I finally found the Lemon Expert. They immediately stepped up to advocate for me.

Bobby Walker, the attorney who handled my case, was sharp, knowledgeable, and a shrewd negotiator. Despite facing numerous challenges throughout the process, Bobby never gave up. He invested the time and effort necessary to fight for the justice I deserved, and his determination paid off. Thanks to Bobby and the Lemon Law Expert team, I am now well-compensated for my ordeal, and I couldn’t be more grateful.

If you’re looking for a law firm that truly cares about its clients and will fight for your rights, I highly recommend Lemon Expert and Bobby Walker. They turned a stressful and frustrating situation into a victory, and for that, I am incredibly thankful!
reviews.ownerResponse: James, we appreciate the thorough and thoughtful review, and we're glad you had a good experience with Bobby! Thank you for choosing us!

reviews.text: THANKS for EVERYTHING Grant

After my car accident, I was in pain, stressed, and completely overwhelmed. I didn’t know where to start or who to trust. Reaching out to the Law Offices of Sandra Castro changed everything. From the first phone call, they treated me with kindness, patience, and respect.

Sandra and her team took the time to really listen to me. They never made me feel like just another case — they cared about how I was doing, both physically and emotionally. They checked in regularly, explained everything clearly, and made sure I always felt supported and informed.

They took care of everything — the insurance calls, the paperwork, the legal process — so I could focus on healing. That kind of peace of mind was priceless during such a difficult time.

I’m so thankful for the outcome they got me, but even more grateful for how they treated me along the way. I truly felt like I had people in my corner. I would recommend Sandra Castro and her team to anyone who needs more than just a lawyer — they need someone who genuinely cares!

Tools

ChatGPT can make mistakes. Check important i

Everyone that I interacted with at the office all had great communication, both in person and through email. They made sure that the process was as painless as possible. Whenever I had a question, they responded to us in a timely manner. I would highly recommend them.
Best experience at law offices of sandra castro #1 For wrongful termination and all kinds of work of discrimination and etc

I used Sandra Castro's office for my case and they did a really great job. Everyone always wants to blame their attorney for there bad case and own life problems. They were very professional and always gave me updates on my case. I'd use them again.

Fast and Friendly service .. Knowledgeable staff everyone is bilingual ... They have been helping my family for a long time
. always with a positive results ... Highly recommended ...

NOT A GOOD LAWYER. FIND SOMEONE ELSE!!!! Why does John have to continue to wait so long for the other company to decide the pay out. It’s going on 4 years now? Our truck is up for repo because we can’t make payments on it anymore. We are going through a very difficult financial hardship.
Sandra Castro is a terrible lawyer that works with conservative doctors and judges, she doesn't look out for the best interest of her clients, so people please beware.

She's a hard working Attorney and she is good at what she does. Her office staff is professional and very respectable  get the job done.

DO NOT USE THIS FIRM! They are unprofessional and disrespectful.  What they advertise is NOT what they offer. Use any other attorney but this firm. If you suffered from what they allegedly serve, don’t waste your time with a consult. Save your precious time and energy by calling any other law firm.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Nicole,

Thank you for reaching out to our law firm. Although we would love to help every individual that calls our office, unfortunately we cannot. Particularly in the area of law that you were trying to retain our services. We understand it can be frustrating consulting with law firms and not getting the response one would like to obtain. We strive to help every caller that contacts us which is why we do not charge a consultation fee nor do we charge people that we do represent, unless we win. Our goal is to provide our community with professionalism, respect and care and we value all feed back as an opportunity to improve. We appreciate you reaching out to our office and wish you nothing but the best in resolving your potential legal matter.

Sincerely,

Kampf, Schiavone & Associates, A.P.C.

I had to fight every step of the way to get anything done. I had to fight to get testing done. I had to fight to get evidence of damage done from work injury. I handled my own case from the beginning and am now stuck in a contract that will ultimately still allow them to receive compensation. But the case isn't over and I will report this firm to the board and fight to ensure they don't receive a penny!!!! You don't care about people and you take on more clients than you can handle.

We had a legal issue, and Heather Cullen and her team handled the matter with great professionalism.  I was quite impressed with how organized and easy our entire process ended up being!  I always hear horror stories when it comes to legal issues, but this was not the case with us.  I believe we got service above and beyond what we paid for. I would call again this office if another issue presents itself in the future. P.S. Jill is the best!

This is my opinion and its based on my experience. I filed for divorce on my own, but then I came to The Law Office of Heather Cullen and Attorney Mrs. Murphy on a recommendation. I did not have a lot of money and told her I wanted to keep the fees to a minimum if possible and get this divorce done. My spouse is being difficult and demands more than her half. I only wanted fair, but this law firm could not get the deal done or even a trial date.  Over several MSC's (Mandatory Settlement Conferences) and a few discoveries, I paid this Law Firm of Heather Cullen and Mrs. Murphy almost $18,000.00 over a period of a year all on my credit card. My divorce is still going. After my last MSC where I understood a trial date could be scheduled, Mrs. Murphy told me they had to schedule another MSC (The 4th). I wanted a trial date, but she couldn't get one. I felt Mrs. Murphy did not fight for me like I wanted. We wasted so much time and money with no results. I fired Mrs. Murphy and this law firm. Then I received an additional bill for over $500.00, which they did not provide an itemized bill. Their billing system is unorganized and unprofessional. Over the course of the year I was with them, I had to ask for an itemized bill more than once.   So I am stuck with all this debt to pay off and still in the same spot I was when I filed for divorce on my own. I DO NOT recommend them. Their fees are ridiculous and over priced for what they did for me.

My experience with this law firm was highly unsatisfactory. I was involved in a No Fault accident where I was rear-ended, and the case initially appeared clear-cut. However, it turned out that the person responsible for the accident had no insurance, resulting in me receiving only the minimum insurance payout.

Regrettably, Mr. Vargas did not show interest in pursuing the individual's private assets to secure compensation for my pain and suffering. Furthermore, I found myself consistently initiating contact and inquiries about the case's progress. Each time I attempted to speak with Mr. Vargas, I was informed that he was in court, forcing me to resort to email correspondence and wait for his responses.

After some time, I was assigned another lawyer from his firm who seemed uninformed about my case, requiring me to provide a detailed explanation of the entire situation, which I found quite absurd. Following this experience, Mr. Vargas resumed handling my case. I had clearly communicated my preference to deal exclusively with Mr. Vargas as my point of contact within their firm.

In summary, my overall experience with this law firm was nothing but a hassle. Despite the impressive appearance of their offices, the service they provided was far from impressive. While their staff was friendly and helpful, I did not get that level of support. I am extremely dissatisfied with the outcome of my case. In my opinion, considering the amount of work Mr. Vargas performed, he did not deserve 1/3 of the compensation I received from my insurance company.

Furthermore, Mr. Vargas billed separately for miscellaneous expenses, significantly reducing the final amount I received. It is imperative to exercise caution when signing the contract and avoid agreeing to additional miscellaneous costs; Mr. Vargas should have received only 1/3 of the reward.

I was in a couple of accidents and was originally with another law firm that wasn’t communicating or making progress. From the first call with Martha, I felt comfortable. She took the time to listen to everything I had been through and was honest about how long cases can take depending on the situation. That honesty right away made me feel good. Even though I live in San Fernando and their office is in Riverside, she didn’t hesitate to take my case.

Martha and her daughters, Adrianna and Nicey, jumped on my cases immediately. They always kept me informed, gave me updates without me having to call them, and treated me like family. Adrianna was also great, patient, kind, and always took time to answer my questions even when she was busy.

What really made a difference was how much they cared beyond just my accident cases. I mentioned to Martha that I was dealing with a difficult personal situation, a family court issue where I lost time with my kids due to poor representation from another attorney. Martha and Adrianna took the time to look into it and helped guide me on how to see my children again. They even helped me with some landlord issues I was having at the time.

This team truly goes above and beyond. They didn’t just treat me like another client, they cared about me as a person. Even though their main focus is accident cases, they went out of their way to help me in other areas of my life. If you’re looking for a law firm that actually cares, communicates, and fights for you, DahDah Law Group is the one to call.

I am appalled at the level of unprofessionalism displayed by Dahdah Law Firm. The communication I received from their office addressed me as “Dear Disciple of Christ,” which was both inappropriate and unsettling. The only way this law firm could have obtained that personal detail was by searching through my Facebook profile—a completely unnecessary and invasive act.

This kind of behavior raises serious concerns about their boundaries and professionalism. Rather than focusing on my case, it seems they invested time in snooping through my personal life to use it in correspondence. Such actions are not only irrelevant to legal matters but also reflect poorly on their judgment and ethics.

If you’re looking for a law firm that handles cases with professionalism and integrity, I strongly urge you to look elsewhere. My experience with Dahdah Law Firm was deeply disappointing and unnerving.

EDIT:
This response from DahDah Law Firm is misleading. Let me clarify: this firm did absolutely nothing to convince me to dismiss my case. It was the claims team from their client’s insurance company who reached out and persuaded me to act in good faith after explaining their client’s circumstances.

Throughout the entire process, this law firm demonstrated significant negligence and a lack of professionalism. From their inappropriate communication to their dismissive and condescending tone, they failed to meet even the most basic standards of conduct. My decision to dismiss the case was made out of consideration for their client’s situation, not because of any actions by this firm.

It’s important that the truth is reflected in this matter, and I stand by my review of their unprofessional behavior.
reviews.ownerResponse:

They're quick to get you to sign a contract, but then when you ask them something, they don't respond. And when you tell them you don't want to continue the case with them, they don't listen, much less return your deposit. I certainly don't recommend this law firm. I've been trying to contact them for a week now and haven't received a single response.

Don't come here! They are quick to sign you on and take your case. They won't start working until you pay their fees (under stable) but once you pay your fees, they just say they have over 100 clients and are too busy. They will ask for documents, and once you send them, they will ask for them again. They don't keep track of anything. This is very unprofessional. Their support staff and management are rude. When you complain they cut you off and argue about how many cases they have. Once there is some change in your case, they will use that as leverage, and say that your case is moving along, even though they don't send you your documents. I emailed them a requested document, and they keep calling and asking for it. You won't talk to a paralegal or lawyer at all. They lie to you and tell you that so that you can sign but once you've paid you're stuck with the support staff who claim are not qualified to give you legal advice. When you request to speak to a lawyer or paralegal, they are always busy, at lunch or in a meeting. For 10k, might as well fill out your application yourself.

reviews.text: DO NOT come here at all!!!! This place has not done anything since we started our case back in November of 2024 for my mom's immigration case. They start you with options and have you sign a contract afterwards, they tell you what documents you need, and at last when you have sent all the required documents, they have you waiting and waiting. You think they would call for follow ups on the case or what paperwork you are missing nope you have to call them. Once we called then they would tell us what we were missing or that there were some errors/problems with the documents we sent. The entire time we are thinking that everything is going smooth and progress is taking place but no false hope. What's the point of charging for the service when they do not even follow up or follow through. We paid them to do their job and be on top of this case, yet it seems like we are doing the job for them. We keep calling weekly for updates and they keep giving us excuses or say that they are still reviewing our case and are putting it together. How long does it take to review a couple of papers? Atleast give us a timeline I know the courts are saturated due to what's going on, but we started this since november of last year and have seen NO results NO progress. They basically pocketed our deposit.

reviews.text: Hello, I'm a resident of Dayton, Texas, and I've been working with these lawyers for almost a year now. I haven't received any response; I've just been waiting and waiting for information. They told me they would contact me every six weeks, but my payments haven't stopped. I'm paying over $600 a month, plus what I pay them, totaling $1700. They said they would cancel the contract with the company that financed my solar panels, but it's been almost a year and nothing has happened. I'm desperate. I also wrote to Attorney Encarnacion, but haven't received a response. I hope someone can help me.
Excellent service even more in Spanish I highly recommend!
Thank you guys for helping me, the process was very easy and fast. Ms Diana and Angela explained everything and walked me thru every step. Thank you, definitely recommend this Firm.

"Complete lack of communication from Defiant Law. I hired them for a solar claim and they have done nothing but ignore my calls and emails. The rep who did my intake is named Santiago, and now the only response I ever get is a text from him telling me to 'trust the process' with zero actual updates.
Under California Rule of Professional Conduct 1.4, attorneys have a mandatory legal duty to respond to reasonable status inquiries and keep clients informed of significant developments. Vague texts from an intake rep do not fulfill this duty. Because they have effectively abandoned my case, I am filing formal complaints with the State Bar of California and the California Attorney General. Avoid this firm if you want real representation."

I have never meet with the lawyer at all and have called multiple times yet I am never directly put in contact with him. It is with his assistant who again is misrepresenting my case as they aren’t even able to answer simple questions.

I did ask for my money back and the response I got back from the assistant was that they don’t guarantee the cases. While I do understand they cannot guarantee cases they did misrepresent my case by not keeping me informed of my case, by giving false information in what their attempts were going to be in making contact with the business and by not being able to respond to questions in a timely manner.

As of August 24 still no response from the assistant…. I came to this law firm to seek justice not to end up being deceived again.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Anonymous,

I’m truly sorry for the frustration you’ve experienced. What you’re describing—especially feeling like you had to chase updates, receiving inconsistent information, and not getting a timely response—is not the level of communication or care we want for any client. You trusted us to help with a difficult solar panel situation, and it sounds like we have yet to meet your expectations. For that, I sincerely apologize.

Because we can’t discuss case details publicly, I’d like to review your file personally, clarify exactly what has (and hasn’t) been done, and make sure you receive a clear, written summary and next steps. If you wish, we’ll also schedule a direct meeting with me, the attorney, to answer your questions and address the concerns you raised about our process.

To make this right, we will:
Conduct an immediate audit of your case activity and communication history, Provide a straightforward timeline of actions taken and options going forward, and Revisit your fee concerns in good faith after that review.

Please email me directly at estefan@defiant-law.com
or call 909-581-4351 with your full name and best contact info so I can prioritize this today. Thank you for bringing this to our attention—we appreciate the opportunity to address it and do better.

reviews.text: One of your attorneys, Joshua Edmondson, has been terrible with communication and isn't helping anyone. He showed up at the courthouse looking very disorganized and messy. I have waited over 3 years to get divorced and this attorney and the firm have not been helpful in any way at all.

Worst law office. I called the office to set an appointment for a "paid" consultation, the gentleman who manages the office took my information and said that he would call me back to set an appointment. A week later, he never called back. When I called again, he said the most gullible excuses for not getting back to me. I went ahead any way and still asked him to schedule an appointment for me to meet with the attorney. He gave me an appointment a week later. He asked for all my information again, which means he did not save my info the first time. On the day of the appointment, I left work early and arrived at the office on time. The same gentleman opened the door and looked confused then he said that my appointment was supposed to be on the phone. I looked upset because he never said anything about having the meeting on the phone. Then he was trying to reach the attorney for over 10 minutes on the phone without any luck. How come the attorney did not call me if the appointment was really on the phone? It was just another lie to cover his incompetence. I just left the office because they lost my trust before I even get to meet with the attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Ms. Thabet,

I hope this response finds you in good health and spirit. First, I like to thank you for taking the time to bring your comments and concerns to our attention. I value the opinions of individuals in our community who in one way or another have come into contact with our office, and strive to create for them the best experience one could possibly expect from a heavily distressful and emotionally-charged period of their lives which is often overwhelming when one must resort to courts or seek legal services in one form or another. I am honored to have maintained an impeccable reputation for commitment to serving clients and non-clients alike with highest level of integrity, respect and attention to their needs. In fostering that kind of environment and adhering to above standard levels of ethics, professionalism and courtesy, I have been blessed with associates and staff who continually strive to give their best to those seeking us out. Our staff training sessions are filled with inspiration, prayers and supplication for blessing and guidance so we can serve the public, and especially our clients, in the best way We realize the availability of many options for choice of legal representation in the Inland Empire, and appreciate the privilege of representing our clients and more often than not, exceeding their expectations.

Having said this, I must confess that despite the use of cutting edge technology to accommodate overwhelming amount of inquiries and request for initial consults occasionally creates a backlog and/or error in scheduling. Additionally, my time in court or many virtual case meetings with my clients may require adjusting the times or rescheduling altogether, and trying to make up for it by offering courtesy fee waivers or reasonable options. As I understand it, there were no fees charged to your card for the scheduled visit to our office, and you did not wish to be accommodated in any other way through a virtual meeting or a rescheduled in-person session. I respect your feelings and decision not to give our office a chance to win your trust in our well-reputed professionalism and sincerity of purpose, and wish you the best in finding legal representation and services that could best serve your legal and emotional needs. I hope you can find it in your heart to be forgiving of innocent human errors that can occasionally occur even o the best of us with the best of intentions.

Sincerely,

Soheila Azizi

And she showed up with wet hair, totally unprofessional! I would never recommend this law firm to anyone!

UPDATE AFTER BUSINESS RESPONDED BELOW:
Their recollection is incorrect, I was not happy with any result of any hearing I had. And I don't know anyone that would be satisfied paying $12,000+ and not receiving any actual advice or help.

YET ANOTHER UPDATE:
I received my copy of the "Notice of Withdrawal of Attorney of Record" and yet I received another bill from their office. This one claiming it is a charge for a voicemail they left me that my physical file was available for pick up. I received no such voicemail and they want me to pay for leaving me a voicemail despite them not being my attorney anymore!
reviews.ownerResponse: This matter was completed, a judgment was submitted by stipulation. Costs vary from case to case and this case extended for more than a year, with several hearings. If I recall, client was happy with the custody and visitation schedule, but did not like the costs. Thats the difference between when you settle and set a matter for trial, require significant prep work, just to settle at the day of the hearing.

Christine James was my ex-wife's attorney in our very messy divorce filed by my ex-wife.  This divorce did not need attorneys. It could have been handled in a very short amount of time.  We had no children together, did not own a home or business together.  On  the day my ex-wife filed, July 1st, 2021, Ms. James office called me, and told me I could go to the house and get my belongings.  I told her office I would be there at noon the same day, they told me they would relay this message to my ex-wife.  When I arrived at my home with a Sheriff Deputy, my ex-wife was angry and shouted at me that Ms. James had not told her I was arriving.  Then, I was never formally served with divorce papers until 3 weeks later, because Ms. James never (or forgot) to serve me properly.  I was dragged through the mud for 10 months. I was falsely accused of domestic violence and abuse, without any proof or evidence.  My ex-wife filed a false, emergency exparte exclusive use of the home order that was immediately denied by the judge because my ex wife did not file a DV report, because again, no proof and no evidence.  Ms. James blindly went along with my ex-wife, not realizing that in the state of California, a DV report must be filed with the court before an Exparte emergency order is even considered.  My ex-wife even stated in her declaration that she had no intention of filing a DV report against me, so you would think that an attorney familiar with the state laws regarding this, would know not to file the exparte order, but Ms. James went for it anyway.  In our temporary spousal support hearing in September of 2021, Ms. James asked for $1200.00 per month from me, at my ex-wife's demand, stating that her client would be "homeless" otherwise.  My ex-wife was awarded less than half.  Later through discovery, as I was being falsely accused of hiding income, it was discovered, through actual proof, my ex-wife was the one hiding additional income which she did not disclose in our support hearing.   Also, before our spousal support hearing started, Ms. James was speaking to my attorney in the hallway, 3 feet away from me, in a very loud voice.  Not professional at all.  All of Ms. James inexperience helped us a great deal. In October of 2021, I was forced to "legally" repossess my 2011 Chevy Tahoe from my ex-wife after multiple attempts of email and phone call communication with Ms. James and her office, with no response.  We tried to rectify the situation peacefully, to no avail.  My ex-wife had parked the  "garage princess" Tahoe outside, took the license plates off, emptied the take of gas, cleaned out her personal belongings, and still refused to give it back to me.   We were told one time that Ms. James had been out of the country, so we gave them one more week to respond, once Ms. James returned.  Still, no response.  So I went and "legally" towed my Tahoe and repossessed it.  It was soon after this that my ex-wife was now representing herself and had either "dropped" Ms. James, or Ms. James fired her.  This could be due to the fact that my ex-wife lied in open court and hid her additional income, I do not know.  In retaliation of me repossessing my Tahoe, my ex-wife filed a bogus, false, domestic violence restraining order against me.  The temporary order was denied, and at the hearing the filing was denied.  The judge actually lectured my ex-wife.  Soon after I repossessed my Tahoe, is when Ms. James no longer represented my ex wife.  My ex-wife is a narcissist.  I have written a book about my life, marriage and divorce to my ex-wife.  It is titled, Goodbye Tammy, Third Time Was Not A Charm, Surviving Narcissistic Abuse, under the pen name Teddy Bowers.  I have also been interviewed on a few narcissism podcasts, YouTube, and I will be featured in an upcoming HBO Documentary called, Empathy Not Included, coming out later this year.

Let me start off with a big thank you to Ms. James and her team for handling my divorce case. I previously was working with another lawyer that scammed me and I was hesitant to work with another lawyer, but I had no choice as my case was challenging.

Let me just say, this firm has high integrity, professional and their attention to detail is impeccable. After struggling for 2 years with my divorce it has finally come to an end and I was absolutely happy with the outcome. Thanks again!

Jessica

reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Buchanan,
 
I appreciate your review because I actually believe it highlights the strengths of All American Law. Before I get to that, I would like to address a few things you have stated while keeping in mind that I am limited in what I can say due to attorney-client privilege.
 
1) This office did not represent you for the entire duration of your 2.5 year court proceeding. Our representation ended about eight months ago. I cannot speak to whatever delays you may have experienced in your case over the last eight months but it is misleading to the public to imply that this office represented you when it did not.
 
2) It is simply not true that you paid this firm $10,000.00. The true amount was far less than that. You should know this from the bi-monthly billing statements that we provided and that you have never disputed.
 
3) You recently contacted my office seeking to re-retain our services, so I struggle to understand how you appear so disappointed with our representation in the past. Only after we declined to represent you again did you immediately post this review. I suspect that our decision not to represent you (as opposed to how we performed when we did) is what motivated you to post this review. Again, this does not help others on this site in their decision whether to hire this office for their own cases.
 
Now to address some other points you made so that the public will better understand the kind of representation they will receive if we were to represent them:
 
All American Law is not a one man show. We are fully staffed with three attorneys, four paralegals, and two administrative assistants. Our attorneys are often in court, but even then if you call us needing immediate assistance you will reach a live person, in the office, ready and available to assist you. We do not use an answering service or automated system. Your urgent matters will receive prompt attention. This also means that when you need assistance but not necessarily the assistance of an attorney, this office can help without charging you at the hourly rate of an attorney.
 
Similarly, you may be better served hiring a different firm if you prefer a lawyer who will call you into the office to chat about things that do not immediately impact your case. That accomplishes little more than to unnecessarily inflate your bill. We do not do that to our clients. Our attorneys will call you when it is necessary for you to be billed at attorney rates. Of course you can set up an appointment to meet with our attorneys if you wish, but we prefer to make your retainer last as long as possible.
 
This former client mentions the case management program our office uses (MyCase) as if it is a flaw when it is actually a very powerful tool for our clients. It provides you access to a personal client portal where you have your entire case file available at your fingertips. You can view your billing statements, events calendar, and documents. You can comment on documents and even send text messages to us for your convenience. Reminders about court hearings and office visits are available. Of course, use of this program is optional. Most clients are thrilled about being able to stay informed about their case in this way, but if a client requests otherwise we are happy to provide case information according to your preferred method such as email, mail, and even fax.
 
Ms. Buchanan, I am sorry that we were not able to represent you again in your case. I wish you the best with your case moving forward and hope that your future counsel will better align with your expectations.
 
- Christine James

I was constantly kept informed about the progress of my case, which provided much-needed peace of mind during a difficult time. Their expertise and experience were evident throughout, and it ultimately led to a favorable outcome.

I can't thank my personal injury attorney enough for their support during this challenging chapter in my life. If you ever find yourself in need of legal representation following an accident, I wholeheartedly recommend their services. They are not just lawyers; they are compassionate advocates who will stand by your side and fight for your rights.

I highly recommend Miracle Law. From start to finish, my experience with them was completely positive. I called Tamar when I was in a car accident last August.

She was attentive, listened empathetically, and explained the whole process. I am glad I made the decision in having Tamar represent me in my case. If you ever go through the same traumatic experience as I did, call Miracle Law for a free consultation so they can address your concerns and needs as they did for me.

You won’t regret it.

Last October I got in a car accident, when the other driver failed to stop at a stop sign and t-boned me. This was the first time something like this had happened to me and I didn’t know what to do.

The next day I called Miracle Law and they immediately reviewed my case and provided me with their analysis. I had never worked with a personal injury office before so I was a little nervous about the process.

However, my attorney Tamar was honest and communicative in each step of the way. I don’t think I could have been more relaxed throughout this process if I had used any other office. She answered any questions I had and reassured me that the concerns I had were normal.

I am so glad that the first thing I did was call these lawyers after my accident and I highly recommend them to anyone who has been injured in a car accident.

My husband and I were rear ended in a car accident on a busy street and I came across Miracle Law’s website. I received a free consultation, where they thoroughly explained the litigation process. We decided to hire Tamar as our attorney and we have not regretted the decision since.

They communicated with us constantly and kept us updated as to the status of our case. If any issues arose regarding the case, they made sure to ask about our input. We felt heard and in control as our decisions were used to move our case forward.

I highly recommend this firm to my friends and family. They provided me with very personalized legal service, which I didn’t feel like I would have gotten at any other firm.

I was recently injured in a car accident and decided I should search for an attorney to see if there was any recourse available to me other than suffering through work and life with a bad back. I noticed Miracle Law offered a free consultation and decided to give them a call. I was put in touch with Tamar and by the end of our conversation I was extremely glad I had called.

Tamar was nothing short of professional. It was super apparent right off the bat that she was extremely knowledgeable in her field. In what felt like just a few minutes she was capable of clearly spelling out to me what potential options I had available. I was astonished at how quickly she was able to recognize and address my concerns and needs. I'd recommend giving Tamar and Miracle Law a call to anyone.

In 2021, I got in a car accident. It was the worst experience in my life, but it could have been even worse if I had not chosen Miracle Law to represent me throughout this journey.

During my free consultation, they explained the entire process and asked me what were the goals I had for my case. I consulted with other attorneys, but I didn’t feel like their services were personalized to my needs.

Throughout the case, attorney Tamar has been very cooperative and attentive. Every time I have a question or concern, they are very accommodating and immediately get in contact with me.

If you are ever in need of an attorney, call Miracle Law.

Rebekah Ryan Main is THE BEST divorce attorney. Full stop 😁. I’d give this woman 20 stars if I could. She is literally Wonder Woman in the courtroom and can handle anything thrown her way. She’s also secretly Mel Robbins in disguise as a divorce attorney, as she always provides motivation and compassion toward her clients.

A little backstory about me: I felt I had one of the most complicated divorces. Prior to meeting Rebekah, I was about six months into the divorce process. It was a contentious divorce from the beginning, with custody issues, restraining order trials, and I was in the middle of an in-progress trial. I was in search of a new attorney, calling around, and no one would take my case. I Googled a million attorneys and asked for the best divorce lawyer. Surprisingly, a lot of attorneys had referred me to the opposing counsel my ex had already retained. They were calling him the Superman of the courtroom, the most difficult lawyer in San Bernardino, etc.

In my search, I’m not sure if it was an act of God, but Rebekah bravely told me she would take on my case. She told me from the beginning that she does minor’s counsel, and her approach is kid-centered and kid-focused. I was scared and hesitant initially as a father of 4 in my divorce attorney search. I’ve heard negative stigmas about males getting a female divorce attorney. When I overheard, “Why would you take this case, Rebekah? He’s not a typical Rebekah client,” I knew I had found the right attorney because Rebekah Main is opposing counsel’s kryptonite. Throughout my case, the opposing counsel was an exceptional attorney in his own regard, but Rebekah is in a completely different playing field.

Rebekah has broken all stigmas I had and is a fantastic attorney. She can go toe to toe with the best attorneys. I can say initially there was a learning curve to get to know one another, but once we did, she has done everything and more. Rebekah is calm, cool, and collected always, and has taught me the importance of mastering patience. She is knowledgeable about ALL aspects of family law. Throughout my case, there was a time where I gave her 1,600 pages of text messages, and she knew them like the back of her hand to use in trial.

Rebekah’s legal staff is amazing as well. I can’t say enough about Hannah, Atziri, and Nicole (sorry if I’m leaving anyone out). Her staff is always friendly and professional. There was a time specifically where Hannah and Atziri hand-transcribed 400 pages’ worth of videos needed for my case.

Thank you to all who’ve worked on my case and for the help, life advice, and words of encouragement you all have given me throughout a very difficult time in my life. To Rebekah, thank you for fighting for me to be a father to my kids. I have definitely learned so much from you. I am still in the divorce process and using her as my attorney, and I just want to thank her for all she’s done for my family. As a father of three young girls, I truly hope my daughters can grow up to be half the woman Rebekah Main is.
reviews.ownerResponse: It has been our privilege to help your family, Erik. Thank you for the very kind review.

reviews.text:
I cannot recommend her enough. I found Rebekah during a long ongoing battle with my ex husband, in which my previous attorneys were just prolonging the divorce and trying to milk us for our money.

Rebekah was upfront and honest and did not sugar coat anything, she let me know from the start that even though I was paying, she was working only in the best interest of my daughter…which as a parents you can appreciate and respect that from a lawyer. She did not add fuel to the fire my ex and I created, unlike the other lawyers, but her and her team got to work to find a solution.

It was thanks to her and her team that I was able to finalize my divorce and custody, after years of failed attempts with other attorneys.

I used her well over 10years ago, but I will always be grateful and appreciative that I found a honest attorney, like Rebekah. Years later, I’m still beyond grateful for getting me out of a hole that I thought had no way out. My daughter and I are together and happy and I’m glad to be done with that chapter of my life.

From day one, Dan Kolacia helped ease a level of anxiety I wouldn’t wish on anyone. Walking into this situation, I was stressed, overwhelmed, and constantly worried about the unknown. So, I used to live in The San Bernardino Mountains in SoCal about 15-20 years ago.  I now live in TN.  I had an opportunity to foster my grandson here.  In the process of being approved to be a foster parent, I was fingerprinted and had to consent to a background check.  I did this willingly believing with all my heart that I would be cleared.  Much to my shock, however, it came back that I had an active warrant in CA for failure to appear on driving without a license.  Now, the reason that this shocked me was because - for the 18 years I lived in CA - I never had a CA license nor did I have a car!  Then, I remembered that my fiance's car, at one point, had been stolen with my purse in it.  We were never able to recover it.  So, whoever stole his car must've shown my ID when they got pulled over!  And, this happened a year AFTER we moved off the mountain.  Anyway, obviously, I needed a lawyer to help me straighten this out.  And, I  I needed a lawyer in CA.  The first lawyer I contacted wanted an unGodly amount of money.  I hung up with him.  The next law firm I called was the Dan Kolacia Law Firm.  The first person I spoke with was Dan's secretary.  She was very personable.  What impressed me is when she said, "I love my boss!". So, I left a message with her for Dan asking him to call me back.  About an hour later, I got a call from Dan.  He was super nice.  He asked me to tell him what was going on and he seemed genuinely interested.  After hearing my story, Dan told me that he thought he could help me.  Then, he quoted me a VERY reasonable price.  I could've cried.  In fact, I think I did after hanging up with him.  Within a short amount of time, Dan had been able to get this active warrant dismissed from my record!!  Shortly after that, my grandson was released to me!  Dan did it all from thousands of miles away!  I would recommend him to ANYONE needing a lawyer!  He WILL get the job done!

reviews.text: I was contacted quickly and got the answer I needed. Very professional and helpful.

Had gotten stopped for DUI, and based on my BAC I was supposed to do 9 months of DUI class, morgue visit, some jail time, and AA meetings. Because I had a clean record prior, He was able to lighten my sentence to only 3mo of classes (which I was able to start as soon as my license was suspended), no jail time, no morgue visit, no AA meetings. Of course it took work on my end to make sure I make it to all my classes, pay my fines and not get into any additional trouble. There was discrepancies on the police report as well, but we could not acquire footage or body recording device to prove otherwise. Definitely don't drink and drive, but if you happen to have a moment of weakness, don't admit to anything, demand a lawyer or else don't answer questions, don't resist arrest, and take care of everything asap!!

reviews.text:
I can't thank the team at Newman & Allen enough for the support and professionalism they showed me during one of the most stressful times in my life. I was falsely accused by my ex-partner, who filed a TRO (Temporary Restraining Order) against me. Thankfully, I found this law office, and from the very beginning, they treated me with kindness, respect, and most importantly competence.

Mrs. Metro represented me, and she was absolutely amazing. She took the time to understand the details of my situation, gave honest advice, and prepared thoroughly for court. Her calm, confident presence made a huge difference, and thanks to her efforts, the TRO was denied. I walked away with my name and reputation intact.

It wasn't just Mrs. Metro who stood out, everyone in the office was wonderful. From April to Ana, every team member was friendly, responsive, and always made me feel like I was in good hands. They answered all my questions promptly, helped ease my anxiety, and walked me through each step of the legal process with patience and care.

Legal issues can be scary, especially when you're falsely accused, but Newman & Allen made me feel supported from start to finish. If you're ever in need of legal representation and want a team that truly cares and fights for you, I highly recommend giving them a call.

Thank you again to everyone at Newman & Allen you helped me get my life back.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Carlos, for your heartfelt review; we’re so glad to hear that Ms. Metro and the entire team were able to support you during such a difficult time and help you regain your peace of mind.

reviews.text: I’ve had the opportunity to work with several law firms in the past, and none compare to Newman & Allen. Their integrity and dedication stand out from day one. Marla provided clear, strategic advice, and I always felt like my case was in expert hands. Xochitl kept everything moving smoothly behind the scenes, and Liz was always so helpful and kind. The whole team is trustworthy, professional, and truly knows the law. I recommend them to anyone looking for solid, dependable legal help.
